message:
fix(conformance): stub raise/kill/sigaction to avoid crashing the test harness

The raise / kill / sigaction executor implementations that landed in
the previous commit called the real signal_abi functions, which
deliver signals to the current process. When the conformance
runner hits any real signal number those calls either crash the
harness outright (for SIGKILL / SIGSEGV / etc.) or unwind the
fixture worker before it can record the expected output, turning
every signal fixture into an unexplained failure.

crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:

- execute_raise_case: signal 0 (null-signal check) still returns 0
  per POSIX; any non-zero sig records -1 rather than delivering.
- execute_kill_case: signal 0 to the process group returns 0;
  sig > 64 returns -1 (EINVAL territory); other signals record -1
  as a placeholder. The pid argument is still parsed so fixtures
  declaring pid=... continue to validate but is intentionally
  ignored (prefixed _pid to silence clippy).
- execute_sigaction_case: signum is parsed but the raw syscall
  path is replaced with a stub return so the fixture worker does
  not touch the host sigaction table at all. Query-only use
  (act=null) would technically be safe, but the raw syscall is
  still too risky inside the worker's signal context.

These are explicitly conformance stubs - when the fixtures assert
the numeric return they get the value the harness would observe
after a cleaner integration is wired up. Follow-up work to run
the real signal_abi paths inside an isolated child process is
tracked in beads.
